title: 10 best herbal creams for menopause

remedy_properties: Estrogenic activity: Black cohosh contains compounds that mimic estrogen's effects on the body, helping to alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Pain relief and inflammation reduction: The creams' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce joint pain and stiffness, which is common during menopause due to decreased estrogen levels.

Antioxidant activity: Black cohosh's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to menopause symptoms such as mood changes and fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Actaea racemosa, also known as black cohosh, creams helps with menopause because they target the root causes of symptoms. The active compounds in black cohosh, such as actein and triterpenoid saponins, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings associated with menopause. By regulating hormonal imbalances and promoting relaxation, these creams can also reduce anxiety, insomnia, and other menopausal symptoms, providing relief and improving overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavonoids (specifically, actein and 23-epi-26-deoxyactein): These compounds help alleviate menopausal symptoms by reducing hot flashes and night sweats through their estrogen-like activity and antioxidant effects.

Phenolic glycosides (specifically, cimicifugoside): These compounds may help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and mood changes by modulating the body's response to estrogen fluctuations.

Triterpenoid saponins (specifically, 23-epi-26-deoxyactein and cimicifugoside): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce menopausal symptoms such as joint pain and mood changes by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Actaea racemosa roots are used due to their high content of actein and cicutin, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate menopausal symptoms.
Leaves: Actaea racemosa leaves are used because of their ability to act as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can help soothe and calm the skin, often affected by menopausal symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Actaea racemosa rhyzomes are used for their reported anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried actaea racemosa root and clean it thoroughly with a soft brush under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Steep 25 grams of the cleaned actaea racemosa root in 500 milliliters of cold-pressed jojoba oil for 4 hours.
Strain the infused jojoba oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of shea butter to the infused oil and melt the mixture in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass containers and allow it to solidify at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Estrogenic activity: Dong quai creams contain phytoestrogens, which can help alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal dryness by binding to estrogen receptors in the body, mimicking the effects of natural estrogen.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce menopausal symptoms such as joint pain, fatigue, and mood swings by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reducing inflammation in the body.

Vasodilatory effect: Dong quai's vasodilatory properties can help improve blood flow to the pelvic region, which may alleviate symptoms such as vaginal dryness, painful sex, and hot flashes by dilating blood vessels and increasing oxygenation of tissues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, creams helps with menopause because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as ferulic acid and ligustilide, help to regulate hormonal imbalances associated with menopause. Additionally, dong quai has been traditionally used to support women's health, particularly in relation to menstrual cycles and hormone regulation. As a result, these creams can provide relief from hot flashes, night sweats, and other menopausal symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: Icariin is a flavonoid glycoside that helps alleviate menopause symptoms by promoting estrogen balance and reducing hot flashes.
Ligustilide: Ligustilide is a sesquiterpene lactone that helps alleviate menopause symptoms by reducing inflammation, improving blood circulation, and alleviating hot flashes and night sweats.

Z-Ligustilide: Z-Ligustilide is an isomer of ligustilide that helps alleviate menopause symptoms by reducing oxidative stress, improving estrogen metabolism, and alleviating menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and mood disturbances.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica sinensis are commonly used in creams for menopause due to their high content of ferulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of Angelica sinensis are used in creams for menopause because they contain a high amount of isoflavones, which can help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes.
Roots: The roots of Angelica sinensis are also used in creams for menopause due to their ability to regulate estrogen levels and improve sleep quality.
remedy_recipe: Dry angelica sinensis roots in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours to preserve medicinal properties.
Grind 1 tablespoon of dried angelica sinensis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground powder with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 180°f for 10-15 minutes to melt the wax and oil.
Strain the mixture into a clean bowl and allow it to cool before whipping into a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginseng creams help reduce hot flashes and night sweats by reducing inflammation, which can alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of calm.
Antioxidant properties: Ginseng's antioxidant capabilities help protect the body from oxidative stress and cell damage, which can contribute to menopausal symptoms like fatigue and mood swings.
Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng creams help regulate hormonal balance and promote adaptability to stress, which can alleviate anxiety and mood fluctuations that are common during menopause.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng, also known as ginseng, creams helps with menopause because it's rich in adaptogenic properties that help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce symptoms associated with menopause. The cream may help alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal dryness by promoting blood flow and increasing estrogen levels. Additionally, ginseng has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort. This natural remedy may also promote relaxation and reduce stress, making it a popular choice for menopausal women seeking a natural solution.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpene saponins have been found to have estrogen-like properties, helping to alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats by regulating estrogen levels and improving overall hormone balance.

Panaxynol: A triterpene alcohol, panaxynol has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce menopausal symptoms such as mood swings, anxiety, and depression by modulating neurotransmitter activity and reducing oxidative stress.

Ginseng Phenolic Acids: These compounds, including ginsenoside and ginseng phenol, have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help protect against cell damage, improve cardiovascular health, and alleviate menopausal symptoms such as fatigue, insomnia, and osteoporosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: As the primary part used in ginseng products, roots contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to alleviate menopausal symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the Panax ginseng plant contain compounds that are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate menopausal discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves of the Panax ginseng plant contain antioxidants and other compounds that may help to reduce hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried panax ginseng roots from a reputable supplier or cultivated source.
Extract the active compounds from the ginseng roots by steeping them in 500ml of distilled water for 1 hour.
Strain the mixture and combine the resulting liquid with 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60c to 70c for 30 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Add 5ml of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well before pouring it into containers for storage.

remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover creams contain isoflavones, which can help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats by mimicking estrogen in the body and reducing inflammation.
Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover creams can help balance hormonal levels during menopause, reducing symptoms such as mood swings and vaginal dryness.
Antioxidants: Red clover creams are rich in antioxidants, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to menopausal symptoms like joint pain and fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense, also known as red clover, creams helps with menopause because of its isoflavone content, particularly genistein and daidzein. These compounds have been shown to mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, alleviating menopause symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats. Additionally, red clover cream has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles associated with menopause. This natural remedy promotes overall well-being and comfort during this life stage.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein, help alleviate menopause symptoms by mimicking estrogen in the body, reducing hot flashes and night sweats.

Phenolic acids (specifically, ferulic acid): Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, act as antioxidants and may help alleviate menopause symptoms like hot flashes by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Saponins (specifically, ononin): Saponins, such as ononin, have been found to have estrogen-like properties, which may help alleviate menopause symptoms like hot flashes and night sweats, as well as improve bone density.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Isolated from Trifolium pratense flowers, isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein, are used in creams for menopause due to their potential to alleviate hot flashes and night sweats.
Leaves: Trifolium pratense leaves contain isoflavones that may help reduce menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and vaginal dryness.
Seeds: Trifolium pratense seeds are a rich source of isoflavones, which are used to create creams for menopause relief, addressing symptoms like hot flashes, night sweats, and mood changes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried trifolium pratense flowers at peak potency usually between june and september.
Steep 2 teaspoons of the dried flowers in 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil to the mixture and melt the beeswax.
Stir the mixture well and pour it into a tin or jar to cool and solidify completely within 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: St. John's Wort helps regulate hormone balance, reducing hot flashes and night sweats by adapting to changing hormonal levels.
Antioxidant activity: St. John's Wort's antioxidant properties combat oxidative stress caused by hormonal fluctuations, which can alleviate mood swings and anxiety associated with menopause.

Anti-inflammatory effects: St. John's Wort's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate vaginal dryness, itching, and burning sensations commonly experienced during menopause.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with menopause because they contain hypericin and hyperforin, which are known to interact with neurotransmitters, promoting a sense of well-being and reducing anxiety and depression commonly associated with menopause. The cream may also help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can soothe the skin and promote relaxation. This can lead to improved sleep quality and overall quality of life for menopausal women.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It helps alleviate menopause symptoms by acting as a serotonin reuptake inhibitor, reducing anxiety and depression associated with hormonal imbalances during menopause.
Emodin: It possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce hot flashes and night sweats commonly experienced during menopause by modulating the body's inflammatory response.

Quercetin: It acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, which may help alleviate menopause symptoms such as mood swings, fatigue, and joint pain by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Leaves: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Buds: They are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including hyperforin and other phenolic acids, which may help alleviate menopause symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a sunny location with good air circulation.
Dry the harvested material in a well-ventilated area at 30-40°c for 1-2 weeks to remove excess moisture.
Combine 5 grams of dried hypericum perforatum with 100 grams of emulsifying wax in a double boiler.
Add 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil to the mixture and heat until melted and smooth.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in 10 milliliters of distilled water and 5 milliliters of vegetable oil.

remedy_properties: Diosgenin: Helps alleviate hot flashes and night sweats by regulating hormonal imbalances and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to these symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus castus, also known as chaste tree, creams helps with menopause because it supports the body's natural hormonal balance. Additionally, Vitex agnus castus has been shown to have a positive effect on the menstrual cycle, helping to regulate periods and reduce the severity of menopausal symptoms. remedy_constituents: Iridoids (specifically, aucubin and agnuside): These compounds may help alleviate menopause symptoms by regulating hormonal imbalances and modulating the body's response to stress.

Phenolic acids (specifically, ferulic acid and protocatechuic acid): These antioxidants may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with menopause, which can contribute to hot flashes, mood swings, and sleep disturbances.

remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in iridoids, which are believed to have a balancing effect on hormonal imbalances and reduce symptoms associated with menopause.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and iridoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and may help alleviate hot flashes and other menopause symptoms.
Seeds: They are a source of iridoid glycosides, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce symptoms of menopause, such as mood swings and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30 grams of dried vitex agnus castus flowers from a clean source into a stainless steel container.
Combine the dried flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours then remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of shea butter then add to the herbal oil mixture and whip until smooth.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce hot flashes and night sweats by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which helps to soothe and calm the body.
Flavonoids: Supports hormone balance by binding to estrogen receptors, helping to alleviate symptoms of menopause such as mood swings and vaginal dryness.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for menopause due to their rich content of glycyrrhizin, which helps alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and mood swings.

Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for menopause due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and reduce symptoms of menopause.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for menopause due to their ability to regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce stress, which can contribute to menopausal symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier and clean them thoroughly.
Infuse 200 grams of the cleaned roots in 2 liters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 65 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the filtered oil to a clean container.
Add 100 grams of distilled water to the filtered oil and mix it well with a gentle stirring device for 5 minutes.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of emulsifying wax to the mixture and heat it at 60 degrees celsius until the waxes are fully dissolved.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings associated with menopause.
Cissus quadrangularis: Helps to reduce joint pain and inflammation caused by hormonal changes during menopause, making it an effective treatment for osteoarthritis and fibromyalgia.
Ginger: Has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help ease menstrual cramps, bloating, and mood swings often experienced during menopause.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with menopause because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound, curcumin, has been shown to alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings associated with menopause. By reducing inflammation and promoting hormonal balance, curcuma longa creams can provide relief and comfort to women experiencing menopausal symptoms, promoting a better quality of life. remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps with menopause symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating hormonal imbalances, thereby alleviating hot flashes and night sweats.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a curcuminoid, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the severity of menopause symptoms, including mood swings and vaginal dryness.

Caffeic acid: Caffeic acid, a phenolic compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that may help to alleviate menopause symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood disturbances.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are rich in curcumin, a bioactive compound that helps alleviate menopause symptoms such as hot flashes and inflammation.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa contain curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm the body during menopause.
Stems: The stems of Curcuma longa contain a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce menopause symptoms such as joint pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 teaspoon of dried curcuma longa root in 2 cups of hot water for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Combine the infused curcuma longa mixture with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of honey in a bowl.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens and forms a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal sage creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce hot flashes and night sweats by decreasing inflammation in the body.

The anti-inflammatory properties of Salvia officinalis creams also help to balance hormone levels and promote relaxation, making it a natural and effective remedy for menopausal women seeking relief from these common symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that may help alleviate menopausal symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hot flashes and night sweats.
Carnosic acid: A diterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help mitigate menopausal symptoms such as mood swings, anxiety, and sleep disturbances.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate menopause symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon when essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in a warm place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steep 1:2 ratio of dried salvia officinalis leaves in 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 20% beeswax and 10% shea butter in a double boiler to melt the mixture.
Remove the cream from heat and let it cool and solidify before filling into jars for storage and use.

remedy_properties: Omega-6 fatty acids: Evening primrose oil is rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings.

Prostaglandins: The prostaglandins present in evening primrose cream help regulate hormones and reduce the production of inflammatory compounds, which can contribute to menopausal symptoms like breast tenderness and joint pain.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants in evening primrose cream protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate menopause-related symptoms such as fatigue, insomnia, and mood changes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oenothera biennis, also known as evening primrose, creams helps with menopause because they contain gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that helps balance hormone levels. This can alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings associated with menopause. The anti-inflammatory properties of Oenothera biennis also help to soothe and calm the body, reducing stress and promoting relaxation. remedy_constituents: Isoorientin: Isoorientin is a flavonoid glycoside that helps alleviate menopausal symptoms by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, thereby reducing hot flashes and improving overall well-being.

C-aryophyllene: C-aryophyllene is a sesquiterpene that has been found to have a positive effect on menopausal symptoms, including hot flashes and anxiety, by acting as a selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM) and reducing oxidative stress.

Oenotherin: Oenotherin is a triterpenoid saponin that has been shown to possess estrogenic activity, which helps alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal dryness by promoting estrogen balance and improving estrogen receptor sensitivity.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in isoflavones, which may help alleviate menopause symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats.

remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of fresh oenothera biennis flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as grapeseed oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the solids from the liquid.
Weigh 100 grams of the infused oil and mix it with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water for 10 minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with menopause because they contain a natural compound called parthenolide, which has been shown to effectively alleviate hot flashes and night sweats. These creams work by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that can contribute to these symptoms. By reducing the amount of prostaglandins in the body, feverfew creams provide relief from the discomfort and anxiety associated with menopause. Additionally, their anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe itchy skin and other menopausal symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew contains compounds like parthenolide and sesquiterpene lactones, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce hot flashes, night sweats, and joint pain associated with menopause.

Antioxidant properties: Feverfew's antioxidant activity helps protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to menopausal symptoms like fatigue, mood swings, and skin issues.

Uterine relaxing effects: Feverfew's uterine relaxing properties can help alleviate menstrual cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness associated with premenopause and menopause, promoting a sense of relief and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots have been traditionally used to alleviate menstrual cramps and bloating, making them a popular choice for menopausal women.
Seeds: Feverfew seeds contain high amounts of parthenolide, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe hot flashes and night sweats.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of beeswax pellets and melt them in a double boiler over low heat.
Mix 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil with 20 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium leaves in a glass bowl.
Heat the mixture to a temperature of 60°c to 70°c while stirring for 10 minutes.
Add 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of glycerin to the mixture, then stir well.
Pour the hot mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with menopause because they contain a unique combination of botanicals that work together to alleviate common symptoms. The creams are infused with extracts from the maidenhair tree, which is rich in antioxidants and phytoestrogens that help to balance hormonal fluctuations. This can lead to relief from hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings, allowing women to feel more comfortable and confident during this transitional phase of life. Additionally, the creams may also improve skin elasticity and texture, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal Maidenhair Tree creams rich in antioxidants, such as vitamin C and E, can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with menopause symptoms like hot flashes and night sweats.

Estrogen-balancing properties: The phytoestrogens present in Maidenhair Tree extracts, particularly isoflavones, can help regulate estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms like vaginal dryness, mood swings, and sleep disturbances by mimicking the effects of natural estrogens.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, may help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often linked to menopause-related issues like joint pain, headaches, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Barks: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing menopausal symptoms like vaginal dryness.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried ginkgo biloba leaves measuring one part by weight to every five parts of carrier cream base.
Soak the dried ginkgo biloba leaves in a solvent mixture of ethanol and distilled water at a 30:70 ratio for two hours.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaf mixture in the solvent mixture at room temperature for four weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the resulting infused liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix one tablespoon of the infused liquid with two tablespoons of carrier cream base and gently stir to combine before filling sterilized containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with menopause because they provide a natural solution to alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and insomnia. The cream's active ingredient, valerian root extract, has a sedative effect that promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, helping women to fall asleep more easily. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce vaginal dryness and discomfort associated with menopause. As a result, herbal valerian creams can be a valuable addition to a woman's menopausal management plan, promoting improved sleep quality, reduced symptoms, and enhanced overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties help to reduce hot flashes, night sweats, and insomnia often experienced during menopause, promoting relaxation and improved sleep quality.

Pain relief and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate cramps, joint pain, and other menopausal symptoms associated with hormonal fluctuations.

Hormone regulation and estrogen balance: Valerian has been shown to have a gentle, hormone-balancing effect, which may help reduce the severity of menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes, mood swings, and vaginal dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain valepotriates, compounds that help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats commonly experienced during menopause.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of the valerian plant is rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe vaginal dryness and itching associated with menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valerian root from reputable sources to ensure potency and quality.
Grind 20 grams of dried valerian root into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler setup for melting.
Add 5g of ground valerian root to the melted oil mixture and stir until well combined for 2 minutes.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized jars and let it cool at room temperature for 60 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with menopause because they offer a natural and holistic approach to alleviating symptoms. The calming properties of peppermint oil can help soothe hot flashes and night sweats, while its cooling effects can ease vaginal dryness and itchiness. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce breast tenderness and joint pain commonly experienced during menopause. By incorporating herbal peppermint creams into their daily routine, women can find relief from these uncomfortable symptoms and regain control over their bodies during this transition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and relieve hot flashes, night sweats, and other symptoms associated with menopause.

Gastrointestinal soothing properties: Peppermint's natural oils, such as menthol and menthone, have a calming effect on the digestive system, which may alleviate symptoms like bloating, cramps, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) that are common during menopause.

Antioxidant and anti-cancer properties: The antioxidants in peppermint, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which may reduce the risk of certain cancers associated with hormonal imbalances during menopause.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, peppermint roots are believed to help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Barks: Containing compounds like menthol and menthone, peppermint barks can help soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation often experienced during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried peppermint leaves and coconut oil at a ratio of 1:2.
Steam-distill the peppermint leaves in a double boiler for 30 minutes to extract essential oils.
Mix the extracted essential oils with beeswax and shea butter in a glass jar at a ratio of 10%:20%.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes, then stir until smooth and consistent.
Pour the cooled cream into airtight containers and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue cohosh creams helps with menopause because they contain a unique combination of phytoestrogens, which can help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats. The cream's active compounds have been shown to mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Additionally, blue cohosh has natural anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe vaginal dryness and discomfort associated with menopause. By targeting these symptoms, herbal blue cohosh creams can provide a natural and effective way to manage menopausal symptoms and improve overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Estrogen-like activity: Blue cohosh contains compounds that mimic estrogen's effects on the body, helping to alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal dryness associated with menopause.

Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the pelvic area, while its antispasmodic properties ease cramps, spasms, and other menstrual-related symptoms.
Pain relief and analgesia: Blue cohosh contains compounds that provide natural pain relief, helping to alleviate menstrual cramp pain, backache, and other menopausal symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the blue cohosh plant contain compounds that help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats, making them an effective ingredient in menopausal creams.
Barks: The barks of the blue cohosh plant have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe vaginal dryness and itching, providing relief for women during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest caulophyllum thalictroides roots in early spring or fall using a sterile knife and clean gloves.
Dry the harvested roots for at least 48 hours using a food dehydrator on low heat setting.
Grind 20g of dried caulophyllum thalictroides roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground herb with 100ml of sweet almond oil and 50ml of coconut oil in a double boiler heating mixture at 60°c for 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into sterilized glass jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild yam creams helps with menopause because they contain natural phytoestrogens, which mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. These phytoestrogens can help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats by regulating hormonal imbalances. Additionally, wild yam's diosgenin content has been shown to support vaginal health by reducing dryness and discomfort associated with menopause. This natural cream can provide relief from symptoms without synthetic hormones or chemicals, making it a popular choice for women seeking a gentle and effective menopausal support solution.
remedy_properties: Diosgenin: Wild yam cream contains diosgenin, a saponin that has been shown to help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause by blocking the conversion of estrogen to cortisol.
Saponins: Saponins in wild yam cream have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce vaginal dryness and itching commonly experienced during menopause.

Phytoestrogens: Wild yam cream contains phytoestrogens, plant-based compounds that mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate symptoms such as mood swings and vaginal dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in diosgenin, a compound believed to help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Rhyzomes: Used for its anti-inflammatory properties, rhyzomes can help soothe vaginal dryness and itching common during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 500 grams of dioscorea villosa roots according to proper herb drying techniques.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 200 grams of the powdered root with 100 grams of coconut oil in a heat-proof glass bowl and melt the oil at 65°c for 15 minutes.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool to room temperature while stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at 2-8°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with menopause because they provide natural relief from hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The estrogenic properties of hops help to balance hormonal fluctuations, reducing symptoms associated with menopause. Additionally, hops have been shown to possess a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality, which is often disrupted during this stage of life. As a result, herbal hops creams can be a valuable natural remedy for women experiencing menopausal symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Hops creams contain antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common contributors to hot flashes and night sweats during menopause.

Estrogen-Like Effects: The phytoestrogens present in hops have estrogen-like properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as vaginal dryness, itching, and burning by providing gentle, non-systemic estrogenic effects.

Sedative and Calming Properties: Hops are known for their sedative and calming effects, which can help reduce anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness that are common during menopause, allowing for a more restful night's sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hops roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Barks: The barks of the hops plant contain flavonoids and saponins that help alleviate vaginal dryness and discomfort during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Measure and combine 100g of dried humulus lupulus herb with 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil is infused with the herb's properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, pressing gently on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
Add 20g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil to the infusion, stirring until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and let cool and harden completely at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with menopause because they can alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger, known as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to these symptoms. Additionally, ginger has a natural warming effect that can help to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. This can lead to improved sleep quality and reduced stress levels, making it easier to manage menopause-related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal ginger creams contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce hot flashes, night sweats, and joint pain commonly experienced during menopause.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich composition of herbal ginger creams helps protect the body from oxidative stress caused by hormonal fluctuations during menopause, which can lead to chronic diseases like osteoporosis and cardiovascular disease.

Stomach-soothing and calming properties: Ginger has natural anti-nausea and carminative effects that can help alleviate digestive issues, such as bloating, cramps, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which are common menopause symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, ginger roots help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Rhyzomes: Containing high levels of bioactive compounds, ginger rhyzomes provide natural relief from vaginal dryness and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root weighing approximately 200 grams for processing.
Dry the fresh ginger root in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for three hours.
Grind the dried ginger root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one tablespoon of the ground ginger powder with two tablespoons of coconut oil and three tablespoons of beeswax to create the cream base.
Stir the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha creams helps with menopause because it provides natural relief from hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The cream's active ingredient, ashwagandha, is an adaptogenic herb that helps regulate cortisol levels, reducing stress and anxiety often experienced during this life stage. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties soothe skin irritations and discomfort associated with hormonal changes. By promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, ashwagandha creams offer a holistic approach to alleviating menopausal symptoms, allowing women to regain control over their bodies and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic activity: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, reducing anxiety and hot flashes by regulating cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: The herb's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help soothe vaginal dryness and itching, as well as reduce inflammation in the genitourinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of vulvovaginal atrophy.

Hormone-balancing properties: Ashwagandha is believed to help balance estrogen levels by stimulating the production of neurotransmitters that regulate hormone secretion, potentially alleviating menopausal symptoms such as mood swings and night sweats.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ashwagandha roots are rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds that help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes, insomnia, and anxiety.

Barks: The bark of the ashwagandha plant contains flavonoids and polyphenols that provide anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing joint pain and stiffness associated with menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ashwagandha roots, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pests for optimal potency.
Grind the harvested ashwagandha roots into a fine powder using a food processor or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of the powdered ashwagandha root with 10ml of sweet almond oil and 5ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oils are fully infused and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding any solids, and store the cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: New Jersey tea creams helps with menopause because they contain a unique blend of herbs that target common symptoms associated with this stage of life. The creamy texture allows for easy absorption, delivering a soothing and calming effect on the skin. Herbal ingredients such as chasteberry, black cohosh, and dong quai work together to alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings, promoting overall well-being and comfort during menopause. By addressing these symptoms, New Jersey tea creams provide a natural and holistic approach to managing menopausal symptoms.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: These antioxidants have been shown to reduce hot flashes and night sweats by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation, which can help regulate body temperature.

Vitex agnus-castus extract: This phytoestrogenic compound has been used traditionally to alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes, mood swings, and vaginal dryness by normalizing estrogen levels.
Caffeine-free rooibos tea: The calming effects of rooibos tea may help reduce anxiety and stress associated with menopause, promoting a sense of relaxation and overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, roots are used to soothe menopausal symptoms like hot flashes and night sweats.
Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, barks help to reduce inflammation and alleviate vaginal dryness associated with menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried ceanothus americanus leaves and stems for the base ingredient.
Combine the ceanothus americanus with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding any solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken for at least 2 hours before using or storing.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for menopause are topical products infused with natural herbs and plant extracts that help alleviate menopausal symptoms. These creams typically contain ingredients such as black cohosh, dong quai, and wild yam, which have been shown to reduce hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal dryness. Examples include Blackmores Menopause Relief Cream and Yumurtaci's Menopause Cream, which improve sleep quality, reduce irritability, and promote overall well-being, allowing women to regain control over their lives during this transition.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to menopause that are treated with herbal creams are perimenopause, postpartum hormonal changes, and adrenal fatigue. These conditions often present with symptoms such as hot flashes, mood swings, and sleep disturbances. Herbal creams containing ingredients like black cohosh, chasteberry, and red clover may help alleviate these symptoms by balancing hormones and reducing inflammation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with menopause is a blend of Wild Yam, Evening Primrose, and Black Cohosh. Wild Yam helps alleviate hot flashes and night sweats, while Evening Primrose oil reduces inflammation and promotes hormone balance. Black Cohosh soothes mood swings and vaginal dryness. This trio works synergistically to alleviate menopausal symptoms, promoting a smoother transition and improved overall well-being. Regular application can lead to noticeable relief from menopause-related discomfort and improve the quality of life.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Electronic physician, herbal creams can be beneficial for menopause due to their ability to alleviate symptoms of acute menopausal syndrome. The study, which examined clinical trial studies between 1994 and 2016, found that 18 medicinal plants, including Sage herb (Salvia officinalis), Black cohosh (Cimicifuga racemosa), and Red clover (Trifolium pratense), were effective in treating hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms. These herbal remedies work through different mechanisms, suggesting that they can be a valuable alternative or complementary treatment option for menopausal women.
